evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment — proactive, hands-on, and ready to take ownership.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Two velocity phrases ("fast-moving startup", "balancing speed and quality") sit alongside rigour signals like "quality checks" and "data people can trust", producing a genuine parity rather than a rigour-dominant JD.
evidence.domain_risk: helping businesses turn one-time buyers into lifetime clients through unique and rewarding experiences
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Payments company but no explicit financial-reporting, compliance, or regulatory language in the responsibilities — errors affect business decisions, not regulated outcomes, so moderate applies despite the fintech-adjacent sector.
evidence.collaboration_width: Commercial; Operations; Product; Leadership; Data team
evidence.data_team_maturity: You're energized by building from zero and shaping new systems and processes turning messy data into clean, usable insights.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: "Building from zero" and "help define and implement our data models" signal the data function is still being established.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain ELT pipelines to support analytics needs across the company.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Some bullets name concrete deliverables (dashboards, ELT pipelines, warehouse layer) while others lean generic ("contribute to building a data-driven culture"), producing a mixed authorship signal.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Be the data partner for Commercial, Operations Product, and Leadership teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Commercial, Operations, and Leadership are named with roughly equal weight and no single function dominates the responsibilities, so orientation is mixed.
evidence.autonomy_level: Help define and implement our data models and data warehouse layer.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: "Help define" and "building from zero" carry strategic ownership of the data layer, but the framing overall is being a responsive "data partner" answering questions from other teams, producing a mixed autonomy signal.
evidence.ai_role: Use AI tools to accelerate analysis, automate repetitive data tasks, and surface insights faster across the business.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to use AI tools to speed up their own analytical work, making this an ai_user classification.
evidence.testing_framing: Set up monitoring, documentation, and quality checks to ensure data you publish is data people can trust.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Monitoring and quality checks are framed as something the candidate sets up and owns, not just a listed tool, so this is a responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: data you publish is data people can trust
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Trust and reliability language appears but is secondary to the delivery-focused framing of the role overall, placing it at moderate rather than high.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Help define and implement our data models and data warehouse layer.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both defines new data models/warehouse layers (greenfield) and maintains existing ELT pipelines and the modern data stack with the Data team (fix/scale), producing a mixed signal.
evidence.language_gate: Proficiency in English and conversational Spanish.
evidence.work_arrangement: Enjoy the energy of our office 3 days a week and the flexibility of working remotely the other 2.
evidence.loss_aversion: Set up monitoring, documentation, and quality checks to ensure data you publish is data people can trust — reliability framing present but secondary to the growth/impact narrative dominating the JD.
